concerns.text: FR-001 and SC-001 require downloading data for ≥95% of subjects, but T014 enforces a retention threshold of ≥95% of the *original* cohort after excluding high-motion subjects. This contradicts the spec's edge case logic which allows dropping subjects with FD >0.5 mm. The task logic forces retention of bad data or fails the pipeline if the clean cohort drops below 95%, leaving FR-001 unimplementable as written.

FR-009 and SC-003 require sensitivity analysis across densities {0.15, 0.20, 0.25}. T019 generates graphs for all densities, but T020/T021 calculate metrics without specifying if they apply to all densities. T031 re-runs the *entire* pipeline for robustness checks. This fragmentation creates ambiguity on whether metrics are computed once for all densities or redundantly, risking inconsistent data structures and leaving the specific output requirement of SC-003 unverified.

FR-010 requires citing the NIH Toolbox validation study. T033 references '(Gershon et al., n.d.)' with an undefined year. Constitution Principle II requires every external citation to be verified against a primary source. A placeholder 'n.d.' cannot be verified, creating a blocking gate violation and leaving FR-010 unimplementable.

FR-002 requires mean framewise displacement (FD) ≤0.2 mm as a preprocessing quality metric. T014 only filters subjects with FD >0.5 mm. There is no task explicitly verifying that the *final retained dataset* has a mean FD ≤0.2 mm, leaving the success criterion of FR-002 unmeasured.

FR-007 specifies a fallback logic: if runtime exceeds the limit, reduce to the *maximum count achievable within the remaining time* (minimum 1,000 permutations). T030 describes monitoring time but is vague on the 'remaining time' calculation and the hard floor of 1,000 permutations, risking an implementation that drops below the required minimum or fails to adapt correctly, leaving FR-007 untestable.

FR-002 requires band-pass filtering (0.01–0.1 Hz). T013 mentions applying nuisance regression and band-pass filtering but does not explicitly state the frequency range (0.01–0.1 Hz) in the task description, leaving the specific parameter requirement of FR-002 potentially unimplemented.

FR-013 requires computing efficiency metrics using Schaefer 400-ROI atlas. T022 mentions 'Schaefer 200-ROI and 400-ROI atlases' but does not explicitly link the 400-ROI computation to the specific robustness comparison requirement of FR-013, leaving the traceability of this specific FR ambiguous.
